note: Machine-checkable validation rules. 'when' gates the rule; 'assert' must hold. Context keys are the resolved mdp settings (hyphens -> underscores) plus system facts.

rules.message: Minimum-image violation: 2*cutoff >= shortest box vector, so an atom would interact with a periodic copy of itself. grompp aborts.
rules.fix: Either shrink the cutoff to 0.49*box, or (better, for an atomistic force field) grow the box — do not drop rvdw below 0.9 nm.

rules.message: PME on a non-neutral box silently adds a uniform neutralising background charge, which distorts the energetics.
rules.fix: Set system.neutralize = true so counter-ions are added.

rules.message: With no bond constraints, X-H bond vibrations (~10 fs period) need dt <= 1 fs or the integrator blows up (LINCS/energy explosions).
rules.fix: Set constraints='h-bonds' (then dt=0.002 is safe) or reduce dt to 0.001.

rules.message: dt > 5 fs is unstable for an atomistic force field even with constraints (needs hydrogen-mass repartitioning, and even then 4 fs is the practical limit).
rules.fix: Use dt=0.002 (h-bonds), or dt=0.004 with mass-repartition-factor=3.

rules.message: Martini tolerates a big timestep but not beyond ~30-40 fs; beyond that beads tunnel through each other.
A minimize stage must use an energy minimiser, not an MD propagator.
A dynamics stage must use an MD integrator, not a minimiser.
Pressure coupling is meaningless during energy minimisation (there is no time, no virial trajectory).
Semi-isotropic pressure coupling decouples the xy-plane from z. That is correct for a bilayer and wrong (it will deform the box) for an isotropic liquid or a globular protein.
The Berendsen barostat does not sample the true NPT ensemble (it suppresses volume fluctuations). Fine to relax a box, wrong for production.
rules.fix: Use C-rescale (or Parrinello-Rahman) for production NPT.

rules.message: No thermostat: this is NVE. Energy drift from the integrator/cutoffs will change the temperature over time.
rules.fix: Set tcoupl='V-rescale' unless you deliberately want NVE.

rules.message: Martini is parameterised with reaction-field electrostatics and epsilon_r=15. Running it with PME/epsilon_r=1 uses the wrong physics for the beads.
Martini's implicit screening requires a relative dielectric of 15.
The solute sits closer to the box wall than the cutoff, so the protein interacts with its own periodic image.
rules.fix: Set system.box_padding_nm >= rcoulomb (1.2 nm is a safe default for a 1.0 nm cutoff).

rules.message: A dynamics stage with zero steps does nothing — sim_time_ns was left at 0.
Reference temperature must be positive.
Temperature above 400 K will denature a protein and boil water at 1 bar. Intentional?
rules.fix: Use 300 K (room) or 310 K (body) unless you are deliberately heating.

rules.message: Cannot both generate fresh velocities and continue from the previous stage's velocities.
rules.fix: gen-vel=yes only on the first dynamics stage; continuation=yes afterwards.

rules.message: A short neighbour-list interval wastes GPU throughput (mdrun will usually raise it for you).
Position restraints are still on during production — the solute cannot move, so RMSD/RMSF will be artificially tiny.
rules.fix: Set posres_fc_kj=0 in the production stage.

rules.message: Salt concentration cannot be negative.
With the Verlet scheme GROMACS expects rcoulomb == rvdw; unequal values are silently overridden or rejected by grompp.
